iterations.theme: Osobni progon, korupcija i prijetnja kleropolitičkog islamizma u BiH

sections.subtitle: Fatmir Alispahić: Bijeg od progona i prijetnji u BiH
sections.screenshot_description: Voditelj predstavlja Fatmira Alispahića, novinara i publicista iz Tuzle, koji je pobjegao iz BiH zbog prijetnji.
sections.content: Uvod u razgovor s novinarom i publicistom Fatmirom Alispahićem iz Tuzle, **Voditelj** ga predstavlja kao kontroverznu, ali važnu figuru koja je nedavno dospjela u medije izjavom da bježi iz Bosne i Hercegovine jer se boji za svoj život. Alispahić, autor 26 knjiga, od kojih sedam o Tuzli, ističe da njegova priča nije samo osobna, već predstavlja **paradigmatsku formu** koja može imati šire negativne posljedice, pa čak i utjecati na hrvatske nacionalne interese. Njegova situacija, kako kaže, gora je od one Josefa K. iz Kafkinog 'Procesa', jer mu je oduzeta sloboda kretanja u vlastitom gradu.
sections.subtitle_en: Fatmir Alispahić: Flight from persecution and threats in BiH
sections.screenshot_description_en: The host introduces Fatmir Alispahić, a journalist and publicist from Tuzla, who fled from BiH due to threats.
sections.content_en: Introduction to the conversation with journalist and publicist Fatmir Alispahić from Tuzla, **The host** introduces him as a controversial but important figure who recently came into the media with the statement that he is fleeing Bosnia and Herzegovina because he fears for his life. Alispahić, author of 26 books, seven of which are about Tuzla, points out that his story is not just personal, but represents a **paradigmatic form** that can have wider negative consequences, and even affect Croatian national interests. His situation, as he says, is worse than that of Josef K. from Kafka's 'The Trial', because his freedom of movement in his own city has been taken away from him.

sections.subtitle: Oduzeta sloboda kretanja i prijetnje islamističkih falangi
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić objašnjava kako mu je oduzeta sloboda kretanja u vlastitom gradu.
sections.content: Alispahić detaljno opisuje kako mu je sloboda kretanja u Tuzli drastično ograničena. Tvrdi da su u gradu uspostavljene **islamističke falange** koje su spremne na nasilje 'u ime partije', uspoređujući situaciju s Hitlerovim režimom. Ne može se slobodno kretati gradom, čak ni otići u šoping, a da ne mjeri prati li ga netko. Iako bi netko to mogao nazvati paranojom, Alispahić naglašava da su njegova iskustva temelj za takav osjećaj nesigurnosti.
sections.subtitle_en: Taken away freedom of movement and threats of Islamist phalanxes
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić explains how his freedom of movement was taken away in his own city.
sections.content_en: Alispahić describes in detail how his freedom of movement in Tuzla is drastically restricted. He claims that **Islamist phalanxes** have been established in the city, ready for violence 'in the name of the party', comparing the situation to Hitler's regime. He cannot move freely through the city, not even go shopping, without checking if someone is following him. Although someone might call this paranoia, Alispahić emphasizes that his experiences are the basis for such a feeling of insecurity.

sections.subtitle: Napad i kontroverzna uloga tužitelja Vedrana Aleđanovića
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić govori o napadu koji je doživio i ulozi tužitelja.
sections.content: U veljači 2024. godine, Alispahić je doživio napad kada su ga dvojica muškaraca pratila, presrela, polila tekućinom i prijetila mu. Kasnije se ispostavilo da su napadači bili **povratnici u kaznenim djelima**, od kojih je jedan osuđivan za drogu, a drugi je pucao u čovjeka s namjerom ubojstva. Šokantno, glavni tužitelj **Vedran Aleđanović** odbio je procesuirati napadače za nasilničko ponašanje, navodeći da je za napad kriv Alispahićev 'drski narativ' koji je 'izazvao' napadače. Ova izjava tužitelja, prema Alispahiću, predstavlja ohrabrenje napadačima i nevjerojatan presedan u pravosudnom sustavu.
sections.subtitle_en: Attack and controversial role of prosecutor Vedran Aleđanović
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić talks about the attack he experienced and the role of the prosecutor.
sections.content_en: In February 2024, Alispahić experienced an attack when two men followed him, intercepted him, poured liquid on him, and threatened him. It later turned out that the attackers were **recidivists in criminal offenses**, one of whom was convicted for drugs, and the other shot a man with intent to kill. Shockingly, chief prosecutor **Vedran Aleđanović** refused to prosecute the attackers for violent behavior, stating that Alispahić's 'insolent narrative' which 'provoked' the attackers was to blame for the attack. This statement by the prosecutor, according to Alispahić, represents encouragement to the attackers and an incredible precedent in the judicial system.

sections.subtitle: Tužba za klevetu i puštanje napadača na slobodu
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić objašnjava kako su ga napadači tužili za klevetu, a jedan od njih je pušten na slobodu unatoč teškom kaznenom djelu.
sections.content: Nakon napada, dvojica napadača tužila su Alispahića za klevetu jer je o incidentu govorio u svojoj emisiji. Na prvom ročištu, Alispahić i njegov odvjetnik otkrili su da je jedan od napadača, koji je prethodno pucao u čovjeka u hrvatskom selu Husino, bio na slobodi, iako je trebao biti u zatvoru. Alispahić ponovno optužuje glavnog tužitelja **Vedrana Aleđanovića** da je 'servisirao' puštanje takvog pojedinca na slobodu. Ova situacija dodatno ilustrira dubinu korupcije i nefunkcionalnosti pravosudnog sustava u BiH, gdje se kriminalci štite, a novinari progone.
sections.subtitle_en: Lawsuit for defamation and release of the attacker to freedom
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić explains how the attackers sued him for defamation, and one of them was released despite a serious criminal offense.
sections.content_en: After the attack, two attackers sued Alispahić for defamation because he spoke about the incident in his show. At the first hearing, Alispahić and his lawyer revealed that one of the attackers, who had previously shot a man in the Croatian village of Husino, was free, although he should have been in prison. Alispahić again accuses chief prosecutor **Vedran Aleđanović** of 'servicing' the release of such an individual. This situation further illustrates the depth of corruption and dysfunctionality of the judicial system in BiH, where criminals are protected, and journalists are persecuted.

sections.subtitle: Institucionalna šutnja i podrška nasilju
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić govori o šutnji udruženja novinara i institucionalnoj podršci nasilju.
sections.content: Alispahić naglašava da, unatoč svim prijavama i dokazima, **udruženja novinara** nisu reagirala niti ga zaštitila. Ovu šutnju institucija naziva 'slikom neslobode' u bošnjačkom društvu, koje se, prema njegovim riječima, sve više zatvara i okreće protiv vlastitog naroda. Osjećaj slobode, koji je pronašao privremeno boraveći u Sloveniji i Hrvatskoj, kontrastira s pritiskom i strahom koji proživljava u svom rodnom gradu. Ova institucionalna šutnja, tvrdi, predstavlja prešutnu podršku nasilju i progonu novinara.
sections.subtitle_en: Institutional silence and support for violence
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić speaks about the silence of the journalists' association and institutional support for violence.
sections.content_en: Alispahić emphasizes that, despite all reports and evidence, **associations of journalists** did not react nor protect him. He calls this silence of institutions a 'picture of unfreedom' in Bosniak society, which, according to him, is increasingly closing itself off and turning against its own people. The feeling of freedom, which he found while temporarily staying in Slovenia and Croatia, contrasts with the pressure and fear he experiences in his hometown. This institutional silence, he claims, represents tacit support for violence and persecution of journalists.

sections.subtitle: Policijski upad u dom i oduzimanje novinarske opreme
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić opisuje policijski upad u njegov dom i oduzimanje mobitela i studijske opreme.
sections.content: Alispahić je doživio i policijski upad u svoj dom. Nakon što je prijavio planirani napad na svoju kuću, policija ga je pozvala na razgovor. Umjesto da istraže prijetnju, oduzeli su mu mobitel kako bi otkrili njegov izvor informacija. To je bio drugi put u godinu dana da mu je tužiteljstvo oduzelo mobitel. Još drastičniji incident dogodio se u prosincu prošle godine, kada je po nalogu tužitelja Aleđanovića **deset policajaca upalo u njegov dom**, pretražujući sve 'do donjeg veša' i oduzevši kompletnu studijsku opremu. Cilj je bio onemogućiti rad njegove emisije 'Defter Hefte', koja je imala veliku gledanost i pratitelje.
sections.subtitle_en: Police raid into home and seizure of journalistic equipment
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić describes the police raid into his home and the seizure of his mobile phone and studio equipment.
sections.content_en: Alispahić also experienced a police raid on his home. After he reported a planned attack on his house, the police called him for an interview. Instead of investigating the threat, they confiscated his mobile phone to discover his source of information. This was the second time in a year that the prosecution had confiscated his mobile phone. An even more drastic incident occurred last December, when, by order of prosecutor Aleđanović, **ten police officers raided his home**, searching everything 'down to his underwear' and confiscating complete studio equipment. The goal was to disable the work of his show 'Defter Hefte', which had high viewership and followers.

sections.subtitle: Korupcija MUP-a i trgovina djecom: Stvarni razlog progona
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić objašnjava da je razlog upada bio intervju o trgovini djecom i korupciji MUP-a.
sections.content: Pravi razlog policijskog upada i oduzimanja opreme bio je Alispahićev intervju s djedom žrtava trgovine ljudima. Alispahić tvrdi da je **MUP Tuzlanskog kantona** organizirao prostituciju dviju maloljetnih djevojčica. Postavio je ključno pitanje: gdje su bile djevojčice dok je tužiteljstvo 'istraživalo', sugerirajući da je sustav možda sudjelovao u podvođenju djevojčica. Upad policije u njegov dom, gdje su se nalazili njegova 91-godišnja majka (koja je preminula mjesec dana kasnije od poniženja) i operirana supruga, bio je traumatičan događaj. Njegova majka, ugledna ginekologinja i sveučilišna profesorica, nije mogla podnijeti poniženje koje je naneseno njihovoj obitelji.
sections.subtitle_en: Corruption of the Ministry of Interior and child trafficking: The real reason for persecution
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić explains that the reason for the raid was an interview about child trafficking and the corruption of the MUP.
sections.content_en: The real reason for the police raid and equipment seizure was Alispahić's interview with the grandfather of human trafficking victims. Alispahić claims that the **MUP Tuzlanskog kantona** organized the prostitution of two underage girls. He posed a key question: where were the girls while the prosecution was 'investigating', suggesting that the system might have participated in pimping the girls. The police raid on his home, where his 91-year-old mother (who passed away a month later from humiliation) and his operated wife were located, was a traumatic event. His mother, a respected gynecologist and university professor, could not bear the humiliation inflicted upon their family.
keywords_en: Ministry of Internal Affairs of Tuzla Canton
entities_en: Ministry of Internal Affairs of Tuzla Canton

sections.subtitle: Optužbe ministra obrane Zukana Heleza i prijetnje smrću
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić govori o optužbama ministra obrane Zukana Heleza i prijetnjama smrću koje su uslijedile.
sections.content: Situacija je eskalirala kada je ministar obrane BiH, **Zukan Helez** (SDP), na svom Facebook profilu objavio da Fatmir Alispahić 'negira zločine nad bošnjačkim narodom'. Alispahić to oštro demantira, navodeći svoj opsežan rad na dokumentiranju genocida i zločina nad Bošnjacima, uključujući autorstvo Virtualnog muzeja genocida, emisije 'Kultura sjećanja' i knjiga o civilnim žrtvama Tuzle i Vukovara. Helezova objava potaknula je **izljeve mržnje i prijetnje smrću** na društvenim mrežama. Unatoč obraćanju SDP-u, ombudsmenima i udruženjima novinara, uslijedila je 'institucionalna šutnja', što je Alispahić protumačio kao podršku nasilju. Zbog toga je, na savjet supruge, privremeno napustio BiH i otišao u Sloveniju i Hrvatsku.
sections.subtitle_en: Accusations of Minister of Defense Zukan Helez and death threats
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić speaks about the accusations of Minister of Defense Zukan Helez and the death threats that followed.
sections.content_en: The situation escalated when the Minister of Defense of BiH, **Zukan Helez** (SDP), published on his Facebook profile that Fatmir Alispahić 'denies crimes against the Bosniak people'. Alispahić sharply denies this, citing his extensive work on documenting genocide and crimes against Bosniaks, including the authorship of the Virtual Museum of Genocide, the show 'Culture of Remembrance' and books about civilian victims of Tuzla and Vukovar. Helez's post provoked **outbursts of hatred and death threats** on social networks. Despite addressing the SDP, ombudsmen, and journalists' associations, an 'institutional silence' followed, which Alispahić interpreted as support for violence. Because of this, on his wife's advice, he temporarily left BiH and went to Slovenia and Croatia.

sections.subtitle: Zatvorenost bošnjačkog društva: Usporedba s Hrvatskom i Srbijom
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić uspoređuje otvorenost društava u BiH, ističući zatvorenost bošnjačkog.
sections.content: **Voditelj** postavlja pitanje bi li Alispahić doživio sličan progon u Hrvatskoj ili Srbiji. Alispahić objašnjava da su hrvatsko i srpsko društvo u BiH 'konektirani' s Zagrebom i Beogradom, što ih čini 'prozračnijima' i omogućuje više kritike. Nasuprot tome, **bošnjački prostor postaje sve zatvoreniji** i, kako tvrdi, usmjeren protiv vlastitog naroda. Alispahić je pokušao potaknuti bošnjačke političare da posjete Grabovicu, mjesto stradanja hrvatskih civila, baš kao što hrvatske delegacije posjećuju Ahmiće. Smatra da je važno priznati sve žrtve, bez obzira na etničku pripadnost, i da je to put ka pomirenju, što je i sam pokazao posjetom Grabovici sa svojom djecom.
sections.subtitle_en: Closedness of Bosniak society: Comparison with Croatia and Serbia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić compares the openness of societies in BiH, highlighting the closedness of the Bosniak one.
sections.content_en: **The host** asks the question whether Alispahić would experience similar persecution in Croatia or Serbia. Alispahić explains that Croatian and Serbian society in BiH are 'connected' with Zagreb and Belgrade, which makes them 'more transparent' and allows for more criticism. In contrast, **Bosniak space is becoming increasingly closed** and, as he claims, directed against its own people. Alispahić tried to encourage Bosniak politicians to visit Grabovica, a place of suffering for Croatian civilians, just as Croatian delegations visit Ahmići. He believes that it is important to recognize all victims, regardless of ethnic affiliation, and that this is the path to reconciliation, which he himself demonstrated by visiting Grabovica with his children.

sections.subtitle: Kritika kleropolitike i turskog utjecaja: Put u 'rezervat'
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić kritizira bošnjačku kleropolitiku i turski utjecaj, koji Bošnjake vode u 'rezervat'.
sections.content: Alispahić oštro kritizira **bošnjačku kleropolitiku** i 'perfidnu' ulogu međunarodnih zvaničnika koji vjerskog poglavara, **Reis-ul-ulemu Kavazovića**, tretiraju kao predstavnika svih Bošnjaka, iako većina nije prakticirajuća. Smatra da je ključni trenutak bio 2017. godina, kada su Bošnjaci, pod utjecajem **Turske i Erdoğana**, odustali od tužbe za genocid i agresiju pred Svjetskim sudom pravde. Taj 'dil' je, prema Alispahiću, omogućio Turskoj da ostvari svoje neo-otomanske ambicije na Balkanu, a Srbiji da preko svojih agenata u pravosuđu stvori 'fildžan državu' ili 'tursku neo-otomansku koloniju' za Bošnjake. Na gubitku je, tvrdi, isključivo bošnjački narod, koji se na taj način gura u 'rezervat', riskirajući da postane 'drugi Palestinci'.
sections.subtitle_en: Critique of cleropolitics and Turkish influence: Path to 'reservation'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić criticizes Bosnian cleropolitics and Turkish influence, which lead Bosniaks into a 'reservation'.
sections.content_en: Alispahić sharply criticizes **Bosniak cleropolitics** and the 'perfidious' role of international officials who treat the religious leader, **Reis-ul-ulema Kavazović**, as a representative of all Bosniaks, even though the majority are not practicing. He believes that the key moment was the year 2017, when Bosniaks, under the influence of **Turkey and Erdoğan**, withdrew the lawsuit for genocide and aggression before the International Court of Justice. That 'deal', according to Alispahić, enabled Turkey to realize its neo-Ottoman ambitions in the Balkans, and Serbia, through its agents in the judiciary, to create a 'fildžan state' or 'Turkish neo-Ottoman colony' for Bosniaks. At a loss, he claims, is exclusively the Bosniak people, who are thus pushed into a 'reservation', risking to become 'second Palestinians'.

sections.subtitle: Guranje Hrvata u treći entitet: Strategija kleropolitičke mafije
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić objašnjava kako bošnjačka kleropolitika gura Hrvate u treći entitet kako bi stvorila 'rezervat' za Bošnjake.
sections.content: Alispahić iznosi tezu da **bošnjačka kleropolitika** namjerno tjera Hrvate iz Sarajeva i gura ih u treći entitet. Cilj je, kako kaže, da Hrvati sami stvore 'fildžanistički entitet' za Bošnjake, a da se onda Bošnjaci mogu pravdati da 'nisu krivi' za odlazak Hrvata. Ova kleropolitička mafija, tvrdi Alispahić, ne mari za narod, jer ima 'rezervnu domovinu' i nekretnine u Turskoj, gdje će otići kada situacija postane 'nesnošljiva'. Upozorava da će bošnjački narod, ako završi u takvom 'rezervatu', ostati bez mogućnosti popravnog ispita i postati 'drugi Palestinci'. Smatra da je obnova mostova s Hrvatima i uvažavanje njihovih legitimnih zahtjeva ključno za europsku budućnost Bošnjaka.
sections.subtitle_en: The pushing of Croats into the third entity: Strategy of the clerico-political mafia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić explains how Bosniak cleropolitics pushes Croats into a third entity in order to create a 'reserve' for Bosniaks.
sections.content_en: Alispahić puts forward the thesis that **Bosniak cleropolitics** deliberately drives Croats out of Sarajevo and pushes them into a third entity. The goal is, as he says, that Croats themselves create a 'fildžan entity' for Bosniaks, and that then Bosniaks can justify that they 'are not guilty' for the departure of Croats. This cleropolitical mafia, Alispahić claims, does not care for the people, because it has a 'reserve homeland' and real estate in Turkey, where they will go when the situation becomes 'unbearable'. He warns that the Bosniak people, if they end up in such a 'reservation', will remain without the possibility of a retake exam and become 'second Palestinians'. He believes that the renewal of bridges with Croats and the respect of their legitimate demands is crucial for the European future of Bosniaks.

sections.subtitle: Stvarna opasnost za Bošnjake: Unutarnja kleropolitička mafija
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić naglašava da prava opasnost za Bošnjake dolazi od njihove vlastite kleropolitičke mafije, a ne od Srba ili Hrvata.
sections.content: Alispahić kategorički tvrdi da **Bošnjacima više ne prijeti opasnost od Srba i Hrvata**, već isključivo od vlastite **kleropolitičke mafije**. Ta mafija, kaže, želi vladati poput Putina ili Erdoğana, a iza sebe već ima niz nerazjašnjenih političkih ubojstava. Pod zaštitom su beogradskih obavještajaca, kojima odgovara bošnjački separatizam jer 'što je više filigranske države, to je stabilnija Republika Srpska'. Kao primjer manipulacije, navodi 'misteriozne pojave' poput žena koje su 'glumile' molitvu u Širokom Brijegu, s ciljem izazivanja međunacionalnih tenzija i guranja Bošnjaka u ekstremizam. Tvrdi da su takve pojave 'teatar' koji Bošnjaci, nažalost, ne prepoznaju.
sections.subtitle_en: Real danger for Bosniaks: Internal clerico-political mafia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić emphasizes that the real danger for Bosniaks comes from their own clerico-political mafia, and not from Serbs or Croats.
sections.content_en: Alispahić categorically claims that **Bosniaks are no longer threatened by Serbs and Croats**, but exclusively by their own **clerico-political mafia**. That mafia, he says, wants to rule like Putin or Erdoğan, and already has a series of unsolved political murders behind it. They are under the protection of Belgrade intelligence officers, for whom Bosniak separatism is convenient because 'the more filigree the state, the more stable Republika Srpska is'. As an example of manipulation, he cites 'mysterious phenomena' such as women who 'acted out' prayer in Široki Brijeg, with the aim of provoking inter-ethnic tensions and pushing Bosniaks into extremism. He claims that such phenomena are 'theater' that Bosniaks, unfortunately, do not recognize.

sections.subtitle: Sofisticirane obavještajne operacije i medijska manipulacija
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić govori o sofisticiranim obavještajnim operacijama i medijskoj manipulaciji usmjerenoj protiv njega i Bošnjaka.
sections.content: Alispahić razotkriva postojanje **sofisticiranih obavještajnih operacija** koje se provode putem lažnih Facebook stranica poput 'Ponosni Bošnjaci' ili 'Parnija Republike Bosne i Hercegovine'. Te stranice, koje mnogi Bošnjaci smatraju vjerodostojnima, koriste se za širenje ekstremizma i vođenje kampanja protiv kritičara poput njega. Kao primjer navodi fotomontažu na kojoj mu je 'istetovirana' šahovnica, objavljenu samo tri sata nakon njegovog intervjua u 'Slobodnoj Dalmaciji'. Tvrdi da takav 'obavještajno-propagandni aparat' nije postojao prije ulaska Turske u mnoge segmente sarajevskog društva i njezinih ulaganja u medijske projekte. Unatoč botovima i negativnim komentarima, Alispahić primjećuje da se sve više ljudi 'budi' i shvaća da ih 'bošnjačka kleropolitička mafija' vodi u samouizolaciju i 'rezervat'.
sections.subtitle_en: Sophisticated intelligence operations and media manipulation
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić speaks about sophisticated intelligence operations and media manipulation directed against him and Bosniaks.
sections.content_en: Alispahić reveals the existence of **sophisticated intelligence operations** that are conducted through fake Facebook pages like 'Ponosni Bošnjaci' or 'Parnija Republike Bosne i Hercegovine'. These pages, which many Bosniaks consider credible, are used for spreading extremism and conducting campaigns against critics like him. As an example, he cites a photomontage on which a checkerboard was 'tattooed' on him, published only three hours after his interview in 'Slobodna Dalmacija'. He claims that such an 'intelligence-propaganda apparatus' did not exist before Turkey's entry into many segments of Sarajevo society and its investments in media projects. Despite bots and negative comments, Alispahić observes that more and more people are 'waking up' and realizing that the 'Bosniak clerico-political mafia' is leading them into self-isolation and a 'reserve'.

sections.subtitle: Želja Bošnjaka za europskim načinom života
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić ističe da većina Bošnjaka želi europski način života, unatoč slici koju projicira kleropolitika.
sections.content: Alispahić naglašava da **Bošnjaci žele europski način života**, sličan onome koji žive Hrvati, Srbi, Austrijanci ili Slovenci. Umorni su od podjela i stigmatizacije. Tvrdi da 90% Bošnjaka živi sekularnim životom, pije pivo, sluša glazbu i želi europske vrijednosti. Problem je, kaže, što ih predstavljaju oni koji ih prikazuju u krivom svjetlu, a podlacima odgovara da se Bošnjaci vide kao nešto što nisu. Vjeruje da se ljudi bude iz straha da će završiti u 'rezervatu' i shvaćaju da je njihov interes u europskim standardima i vrijednostima.
sections.subtitle_en: Desire of Bosniaks for European way of life
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić highlights that most Bosniaks want a European way of life, despite the image projected by cleropolitics.
sections.content_en: Alispahić emphasizes that **Bosniaks want a European way of life**, similar to the one lived by Croats, Serbs, Austrians, or Slovenes. They are tired of divisions and stigmatization. He claims that 90% of Bosniaks live a secular life, drink beer, listen to music, and want European values. The problem is, he says, that they are represented by those who portray them in a false light, and he responds to the villains that Bosniaks are seen as something they are not. He believes that people are waking up from the fear of ending up in a 'reservation' and realize that their interest lies in European standards and values.

sections.subtitle: Nasilje izbora Željka Komšića i njegove posljedice
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić kritizira izbor Željka Komšića i njegove negativne posljedice na odnose Bošnjaka i Hrvata.
sections.content: Alispahić oštro kritizira 'izborno nasilje' koje se očituje u izboru **Željka Komšića** za hrvatskog člana Predsjedništva BiH, unatoč tome što ga Hrvati ne žele. Tvrdi da je to 'kvazi-građanski' projekt koji je u proteklih šesnaest godina srušio mostove između Bošnjaka i Hrvata i potaknuo opravdane ambicije Hrvata za vlastitim entitetom. Alispahić je još 2010. godine upozoravao da će takva politika dovesti do stvaranja 'hrvatskog entiteta'. Naglašava da nije u hrvatskom nacionalnom interesu imati 'Palestinu' u susjedstvu i kritizira hrvatsku politiku što nije proaktivnije podržala proeuropsku bošnjačku inteligenciju koja zagovara legitimno predstavljanje Hrvata. Smatra da je Komšićev izbor predstava koja gura Hrvate izvan Federacije i BiH, s ciljem uspostave 'Erdoğanovog kalifata' ili 'turske neo-otomanske kolonije'.
sections.subtitle_en: Violence of the election of Željko Komšić and its consequences
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić criticizes the election of Željko Komšić and its negative consequences on the relations of Bosniaks and Croats.
sections.content_en: Alispahić sharply criticizes 'electoral violence' which is manifested in the election of **Željko Komšić** as the Croat member of the Presidency of BiH, despite the fact that Croats do not want him. He claims that it is a 'quasi-civic' project which in the past sixteen years destroyed bridges between Bosniaks and Croats and encouraged legitimate ambitions of Croats for their own entity. As early as 2010, Alispahić warned that such a policy would lead to the creation of a 'Croat entity'. He emphasizes that it is not in the Croatian national interest to have 'Palestine' in the neighborhood and criticizes Croatian politics for not more proactively supporting pro-European Bosniak intelligence which advocates for legitimate representation of Croats. He believes that Komšić's election is a show that pushes Croats out of the Federation and BiH, with the aim of establishing 'Erdoğan's caliphate' or 'Turkish neo-Ottoman colony'.

sections.subtitle: Korupcija u pravosuđu: Stup države pod kontrolom mafije
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić daje primjer korupcije u pravosuđu, gdje se presude kupuju.
sections.content: Alispahić ilustrira duboku korupciju u pravosuđu šokantnim primjerom iz Mostara, gdje je pijani i drogirani vozač usmrtio djevojku. Umjesto pravedne kazne, organizirana je akcija prikupljanja novca 'da se plati sud' i izbjegne velika presuda. Vozač je dobio samo tri godine zatvora i pušten je na slobodu nakon nekoliko mjeseci. Alispahić opisuje pravosuđe kao 'najbolesniji' dio države, koje bi trebalo biti njezin stup, ali je umjesto toga **povezano s mafijom** i omogućuje 'kupovanje presuda'. Svoje iskustvo s korumpiranim sustavom opisao je u knjizi 'Kronika zavjere', gdje je bio optužen za 'nacionalnu mržnju prema migrantima', iako migranti nisu predmet kaznenopravne zaštite.
sections.subtitle_en: Corruption in the judiciary: Pillar of the state under the control of the mafia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić gives an example of corruption in the judiciary, where verdicts are bought.
sections.content_en: Alispahić illustrates deep corruption in the judiciary with a shocking example from Mostar, where a drunk and drugged driver killed a girl. Instead of a just punishment, an action was organized to collect money 'to pay the court' and avoid a large sentence. The driver received only three years in prison and was released after several months. Alispahić describes the judiciary as the 'sickest' part of the state, which should be its pillar, but is instead **connected with the mafia** and enables 'buying sentences'. He described his experience with the corrupt system in the book 'Kronika zavjere', where he was accused of 'national hatred towards migrants', although migrants are not subject to criminal-legal protection.

sections.subtitle: Nema rata između naroda, ali prijeti unutarnji sukob i islamizam
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić objašnjava da ne prijeti rat između naroda, već unutarnji sukob i islamizam unutar bošnjačkog društva.
sections.content: Alispahić smatra da **ne može biti više nikakvog rata između naroda** u BiH, jer su Srbi i Hrvati suvereni na svojim dijelovima teritorija. Kao primjer navodi odnos prema migrantima: Srbi i Hrvati ih nisu primali, dok su Islamska zajednica i SDA otvarale 'narodne kuhinje' i džamije, stvarajući 'parking za migrante' u bošnjačkim područjima. Boji se da će Bošnjaci postati 'muslimanski, afroazijski derivat u Europi'. Stvarna opasnost leži u **unutarnjem građanskom ratu** unutar bošnjačkog društva, potaknutom 'islamističkim milicijama' i 'parapolicijskim sustavom' bradatih muškaraca povezanih kroz klubove borilačkih vještina, koji se mogu brzo mobilizirati. Smatra da srpski i hrvatski kadrovi u policiji i pravosuđu možda dopuštaju islamizaciju iz vlastitih strateških razloga, jer 'što je više fildžan države, to je više hrvatskog entiteta, to je stabilnija Republika Srpska'. Alispahić jasno razlikuje islam (svoju vjeru) od islamizma (ekstremizma), upozoravajući da mali, ali agresivni postotak islamista, povezan s financijskim i mafijaškim centrima, može zaprijetiti institucijama i dovesti Bošnjake do 'nestanka' stvaranjem 'rezervata'.
sections.subtitle_en: There is no war between peoples, but internal conflict and Islamism threaten.
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić explains that war does not threaten between peoples, but an internal conflict and Islamism within Bosniak society.
sections.content_en: Alispahić believes that **there can be no more war between peoples** in BiH, because Serbs and Croats are sovereign in their parts of the territory. As an example, he cites the attitude towards migrants: Serbs and Croats did not accept them, while the Islamic Community and SDA opened 'soup kitchens' and mosques, creating a 'parking lot for migrants' in Bosniak areas. He fears that Bosniaks will become a 'Muslim, Afro-Asian derivative in Europe'. The real danger lies in an **internal civil war** within Bosniak society, incited by 'Islamist militias' and a 'para-police system' of bearded men connected through martial arts clubs, who can be quickly mobilized. He believes that Serbian and Croatian personnel in the police and judiciary might be allowing Islamization for their own strategic reasons, because 'the more a "fildžan" state there is, the more a Croatian entity there is, the more stable the Republika Srpska is'. Alispahić clearly distinguishes Islam (his faith) from Islamism (extremism), warning that a small but aggressive percentage of Islamists, connected with financial and mafia centers, can threaten institutions and lead Bosniaks to 'disappearance' by creating 'reservations'.

iterations.theme_en: Personal persecution, corruption and the threat of clerico-political Islamism in BiH

iterations.theme: Nada za BiH: Sport, europske vrijednosti i poziv na promjenu

sections.subtitle: Reprezentacija BiH: Simbol nade i multietničnosti
sections.screenshot_description: Novi Čušković i Fatmir Alispahić razgovaraju o uspjehu nogometne reprezentacije BiH kao ujedinjujućem faktoru.
sections.content: Novi Čušković ističe kako je nogometna reprezentacija Bosne i Hercegovine, sastavljena od pravoslavaca, katolika i Bošnjaka, postala simbol nade i dokaz da je suživot moguć. Njezin plasman na prvenstvo, prema njegovim riječima, pokazuje da se ipak može postići zajednički uspjeh. Posebno hvali ulogu Sergeja Barbareza, kojeg opisuje kao neopterećenog sportaša koji je uspio ujediniti ljude. Fatmir Alispahić potvrđuje da je riječ o veličanstvenoj priči i pohvaljuje hrvatske medije koji grade dobrosusjedsku euforiju, potičući navijanje za BiH reprezentaciju, baš kao što će se i u BiH navijati za Hrvatsku. Naglašava da je važno držati se državne zastave kao simbola koji objedinjuje sve, umjesto nacionalističkih ili vjerskih simbola, kako bi se Hrvati i Srbi u BiH mogli komotno osjećati navijajući za svoju reprezentaciju.
sections.subtitle_en: BiH National Team: Symbol of hope and multiethnicity
sections.screenshot_description_en: Novi Čušković and Fatmir Alispahić discuss the success of the BiH national football team as a unifying factor.
sections.content_en: Novi Čušković points out that the football national team of Bosnia and Herzegovina, composed of Orthodox, Catholics, and Bosniaks, has become a symbol of hope and proof that coexistence is possible. Its qualification for the championship, according to him, shows that common success can indeed be achieved. He particularly praises the role of Sergej Barbarez, whom he describes as an unburdened athlete who managed to unite people. Fatmir Alispahić confirms that it is a magnificent story and praises Croatian media that are building good-neighborly euphoria, encouraging support for the BiH national team, just as the Croatian team will be supported in BiH. He emphasizes that it is important to stick to the state flag as a symbol that unites everyone, instead of nationalistic or religious symbols, so that Croats and Serbs in BiH can feel comfortable cheering for their national team.

sections.subtitle: Umor od podjela i želja za europskim životom
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić objašnjava kako većina Bosanaca želi europski način života, umorna od podjela.
sections.content: Novi Čušković primjećuje da su ljudi umorni od podjela i nacionalizma, ne samo u Bosni i Hercegovini, već i u Hrvatskoj i Srbiji. Fatmir Alispahić se slaže, ističući da su Bosanci umorni od stigmatizacije i pogrešnog predstavljanja. Naglašava da je **Bošnjak europski građanin** i da 90% Bošnjaka živi jednako kao i Hrvati, Srbi, Austrijanci ili Slovenci – uživajući u druženjima, vinu, pivu i glazbi. Smatra da oni koji predstavljaju Bošnjake to čine u krivom svjetlu, a da je podlacima stalo da se Bošnjaci vide onakvima kakvi nisu. Kritizira ideju da vjerski poglavar, poput reisu-l-uleme Kavazovića, može predstavljati većinu Bošnjaka, jer on zastupa samo mali postotak praktičnih vjernika. Fatmir Alispahić vjeruje da se ljudi bude i shvaćaju da ih kleropolitička mafija vodi u rezervat i samoizolaciju, dok oni žele sekularni poredak i europske vrijednosti.
sections.subtitle_en: Weariness from divisions and desire for European life
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić explains how most Bosnians want a European way of life, tired of divisions.
sections.content_en: Novi Čušković notices that people are tired of divisions and nationalism, not only in Bosnia and Herzegovina, but also in Croatia and Serbia. Fatmir Alispahić agrees, pointing out that Bosnians are tired of stigmatization and misrepresentation. He emphasizes that **a Bosniak is a European citizen** and that 90% of Bosniaks live the same way as Croats, Serbs, Austrians, or Slovenes – enjoying socializing, wine, beer, and music. He believes that those who represent Bosniaks do so in a wrong light, and that villains care for Bosniaks to be seen as they are not. He criticizes the idea that a religious leader, such as reisu-l-ulema Kavazović, can represent the majority of Bosniaks, because he represents only a small percentage of practical believers. Fatmir Alispahić believes that people are waking up and realizing that the clerico-political mafia is leading them into a reservation and self-isolation, while they want a secular order and European values.

sections.subtitle: Izlaz iz 'rezervata': Prihvaćanje hrvatske ruke i odbacivanje turskog utjecaja
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić govori o potrebi Bosanaca da se okrenu od Turske i prihvate suradnju s Hrvatima.
sections.content: Fatmir Alispahić vidi šansu za Bosance da se probude i shvate da život u 'rezervatu' vodi u nestanak i iseljavanje. Ističe da je **bošnjački interes** da Hrvati ostanu u Sarajevu, a ne da budu izvan njega, jer jaki bošnjačko-hrvatski odnosi jačaju Federaciju i slabe separatizam Milorada Dodika. Smatra da je jačanje Dodika i zatvaranje Bošnjaka u rezervat dio šire strategije, uključujući i izraelske lobije, s ciljem istiskivanja turskog utjecaja iz BiH. Tursku opisuje kao povijesno štetnu za Bošnjake, navodeći primjere prodaje Bošnjaka za vrijeme Austrije i čuvanja leđa Srbiji tijekom agresije na BiH. Upozorava da Bošnjaci moraju pobjeći iz te zamke, jer su u odnosu na Tursku uvijek gubitnici. Spominje i kupovinu i uništavanje velikih tvornica u bošnjačkim područjima kapitalom koji ide preko Izraela, što rezultira kolonijom s trgovačkim centrima, ali bez proizvodnje. Kritizira bošnjačku politiku, uključujući Denisa Bećirovića, zbog neuspjeha u obnovi mostova s Hrvatima. Zaključuje da Bosna i Hercegovina, a posebno Bošnjaci, moraju prihvatiti **dobronamjerno pruženu hrvatsku ruku** jer Hrvati ne bi tražili treći entitet da imaju jednakopravnost i da se ne dira u njihova prava.
sections.subtitle_en: Exit from 'reservation': Acceptance of the Croatian hand and rejection of Turkish influence
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić speaks about the need for Bosnians to turn away from Turkey and accept cooperation with Croats.
sections.content_en: Fatmir Alispahić sees a chance for Bosnians to wake up and realize that life in a 'reservation' leads to disappearance and emigration. He points out that it is the **Bosniak interest** for Croats to remain in Sarajevo, and not to be outside of it, because strong Bosniak-Croatian relations strengthen the Federation and weaken Milorad Dodik's separatism. He believes that the strengthening of Dodik and the confinement of Bosniaks to a reservation is part of a broader strategy, including Israeli lobbies, with the aim of displacing Turkish influence from BiH. He describes Turkey as historically harmful to Bosniaks, citing examples of the sale of Bosniaks during Austria and backing Serbia during the aggression on BiH. He warns that Bosniaks must escape from that trap, because in relation to Turkey, they are always losers. He also mentions the purchase and destruction of large factories in Bosniak areas with capital that goes through Israel, which results in a colony with shopping centers, but without production. He criticizes Bosniak politics, including Denis Bećirović, for the failure to rebuild bridges with Croats. He concludes that Bosnia and Herzegovina, and especially Bosniaks, must accept the **benevolently extended Croatian hand** because Croats would not seek a third entity if they had equality and if their rights were not infringed upon.

sections.subtitle: Svjetlo na kraju tunela i poziv Bošnjacima na promjenu
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić izražava nadu u promjenu, ali naglašava da Bošnjaci moraju sami preuzeti inicijativu.
sections.content: Fatmir Alispahić vidi svjetlo na kraju tunela u probijanju medijskih blokada i otvorenim razgovorima, zahvaljujući, između ostalog, i ovom podcastu te medijima poput Slobodne Dalmacije. Ističe da postoje iskre dobrohotnosti koje bi mogle spasiti Bošnjake, ali naglašava da **Bošnjaci moraju sami raditi na tome**, a ne očekivati da to čine Hrvati za njih. Poziva Bošnjake da nakon šesnaest godina prestanu 'doticati hrvatsko dvorište', odnosno miješati se u legitimno predstavljanje Hrvata. Kritizira bošnjačke predstavnike koji, po njegovom mišljenju, ne mare za nestanak Bošnjaka, već im je cilj opljačkati što više i pobjeći u Tursku, koju smatraju rezervnom domovinom. Vjeruje da bi prihvaćanje realnosti i suradnja s Hrvatima riješili mnoge probleme, uključujući i ruski utjecaj na Republiku Srpsku, jačanjem jedinstva Federacije i države BiH.
sections.subtitle_en: Light at the end of the tunnel and a call to Bosniaks to change
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić expresses hope for change, but emphasizes that Bosniaks must themselves take the initiative.
sections.content_en: Fatmir Alispahić sees light at the end of the tunnel in breaking through media blockades and open conversations, thanks, among other things, to this podcast and media like Slobodna Dalmacija. He points out that there are sparks of goodwill that could save Bosniaks, but emphasizes that **Bosniaks must work on it themselves**, and not expect Croats to do it for them. He calls on Bosniaks to stop 'touching the Croatian yard' after sixteen years, i.e., interfering in the legitimate representation of Croats. He criticizes Bosniak representatives who, in his opinion, do not care about the disappearance of Bosniaks, but whose goal is to plunder as much as possible and flee to Turkey, which they consider a reserve homeland. He believes that accepting reality and cooperation with Croats would solve many problems, including Russian influence on Republika Srpska, by strengthening the unity of the Federation and the state of BiH.
keywords_en: light at the end of the tunnel

sections.subtitle: Prijedlog glasanja za HDZ kandidatkinju i završne riječi
sections.screenshot_description: Fatmir Alispahić iznosi kontroverzan prijedlog da Bošnjaci glasaju za kandidatkinju HDZ-a.
sections.content: U kontekstu nedostatka pravog izbora među bošnjačkim kandidatima za Predsjedništvo BiH, Fatmir Alispahić iznosi radikalan prijedlog: da Bošnjaci glasaju za kandidatkinju HDZ-a. Smatra da bi se time 'iskupili' i osigurali da najjača hrvatska stranka ima svog predstavnika u Predsjedništvu Bosne i Hercegovine. Izjavljuje da on osobno neće glasati ni za Bakira Izetbegovića ni za Denisa Bećirovića, već za HDZ-ovu kandidatkinju. Novi Čušković se na kraju razgovora osvrće na ranije spomenutu 'tetovažu' (fotomontažu šahovnice), na što Fatmir Alispahić šaljivo odgovara da tajna treba ostati tajna, zaključujući razgovor u opuštenijem tonu.
sections.subtitle_en: Proposal for voting for the HDZ candidate and final words
sections.screenshot_description_en: Fatmir Alispahić presents a controversial proposal that Bosniaks vote for the HDZ candidate.
sections.content_en: In the context of a lack of real choice among Bosniak candidates for the Presidency of BiH, Fatmir Alispahić puts forward a radical proposal: that Bosniaks vote for the HDZ candidate. He believes that by doing so they would 'redeem themselves' and ensure that the strongest Croatian party has its representative in the Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina. He declares that he personally will not vote for Bakir Izetbegović nor for Denis Bećirović, but for the HDZ candidate. At the end of the conversation, Novi Čušković refers to the previously mentioned 'tattoo' (a photomontage of the checkerboard), to which Fatmir Alispahić jokingly replies that the secret should remain a secret, concluding the conversation in a more relaxed tone.

iterations.theme_en: Hope for BiH: Sport, European values and a call for change
